title: feat: add Laguna S 2.1
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds the paid-only `laguna` service with `laguna-s-2.1`, `laguna-s2.1`, and `poolside-laguna-s-2.1` aliases.
- Pins OpenRouter to Poolside's `poolside/laguna-s-2.1` endpoint with provider fallbacks disabled.
- Registers text input/output, tools, optional reasoning, 1,048,576-token context, and exact $0.10/M input, $0.01/M cached-input, $0.20/M output rates.
- Clarifies that model descriptions are user-facing developer catalog copy and keeps unsupported image/JSON-schema capabilities unadvertised.

Tests:
- 279 focused Enter tests and 103 focused Gen tests
- Gen and Enter TypeScript checks
- Local E2E: canonical name + aliases, non-stream + stream, tools + continuation, reasoning, cache, paid-only gate, malformed inputs, usage headers, and Tinybird billing parity

gist.headline: Laguna joins the hive
gist.blurb: A new paid-only lagoon has appeared in the model garden. Laguna S 2.1 brings a very large context window, tools, and optional reasoning—without pretending it can paint pixels or speak JSON schemas.
gist.summary: Added the paid-only Poolside Laguna S 2.1 text model, available as `laguna`, `laguna-s2.1`, and `poolside-laguna-s-2.1`, with tools, optional reasoning, a 1,048,576-token context window, and pinned OpenRouter routing to `poolside/laguna-s-2.1`.
gist.impact: Developers can now use Laguna S 2.1 through the existing text API for million-token-context conversations and tool workflows, with exact rates of $0.10/M input, $0.01/M cached input, and $0.20/M output.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Laguna as a calm pastel lagoon in the pollinations model garden: a huge scroll of code and conversation winding around its shore represents the 1,048,576-token context window, while small tool icons rest on lily pads. Polli, wearing a tiny explorer hat and holding a map, guides the eager CRT Monitor Robot toward a sign reading “LAGUNA S 2.1”; code-plants and flowers make the new model feel like a discovered part of the ecosystem.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink cheek blush and light blue wings, wearing a tiny explorer hat and holding a map beside a serene pastel lagoon, where an enormous scrolling code parchment curls around the water to represent long context and little tool icons float on lily pads; an eager chunky CRT monitor robot points at a pixel sign reading “LAGUNA S 2.1.” Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel mint-lavender-peach gradient background, code-plants and flowers along the shore, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, nostalgic but beautiful and emotionally warm.
